mission.title: traefik: Please disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 by default
mission.description: Please disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 by default. This issue affects 66+ users.

steps.title: Identify traefik upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your traefik deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik -n traefik --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
Using TLS 1.0 or 1.1 is considered insecure these days. It should be used only by those who really know what they're doing. For that reason, I suggest disabling TLS 1.0 and 1.1 by default.

I'm on Traefik 2.2.1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Please disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 by default
steps.description:
### What does this PR do?

Change the default TLS Options for more security while maintaining compatibility with most clients

### Motivation

https://github.com/golang/go/issues/45428

Go will remove support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1 soon, so I think it's better if we prepare in advance by changing the
```yaml
tls:
  options:
    default:
      minVersion: VersionTLS12
      cipherSuites:
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305
```

steps.title: Confirm Please disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 by default is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik -n traefik --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
### What does this PR do?

Change the default TLS Options for more security while maintaining compatibility with most clients

### Motivation

https://github.com/golang/go/issues/45428

Go will remove support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1 soon, so I think it's better if we prepare in advance by changing the default values and then adding a notice on the deprecations page.
codeSnippets:
tls:
  options:
    default:
      minVersion: VersionTLS12
      cipherSuites:
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305

image: "traefik:v2.2.8"
 tls:
   options:
     default:
       minVersion: VersionTLS12
       cipherSuites:
         - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
         - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
         - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
         - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
         - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305
         - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305
 restart: always

$ cat traefik/traefik.dynamic.yaml
tls:
  options:
    default:
      minVersion: VersionTLS12
      cipherSuites:
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
        - TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
